content: Phase II trials are the first studies to evaluate drug efficacy in patients with the target disease, while continuing safety assessment. These trials typically enroll 100-300 patients and last several months to 2 years. Phase II is divided into IIa (proof-of-concept, demonstrating biological activity) and IIb (dose-finding, establishing optimal dosing). The primary goal is to determine whether the drug shows sufficient efficacy signal to justify large-scale Phase III testing. Approximately 30-40% of drugs successfully advance from Phase II to Phase III.
